_comment.1: SOUNDCARD CHARACTERIZATION bode: command -> soundcard line-out ->
_comment.2: (analog summing) -> FSM -> camera -> CoM, x/tilt axis. Same method as
_comment.3: conf_bode_x.json but the ACTUATOR is anyloop:aylp_alsa instead of the
_comment.4: piplate. bode_plot replaces the x element (1; CoM is [y,x]) with a sine;
_comment.5: aylp_alsa writes it to the sound card (scale [0,-1] = drive x only, sign
_comment.6: mirrors the piplate's ch1 -2). The piplate STAYS in the pipeline with
_comment.7: scale [0,0,0,0] -- it drives nothing, it only holds the DC bias offset
_comment.8: (2 V) so the FSM sits in its linear range while the AC-coupled soundcard
_comment.9: does the sweep. So the measured |H| is the PURE soundcard->FSM path.
_comment.10: WHAT TO READ OFF THE PDF:
_comment.11: * AC HIGH-PASS CORNER: |H| rolls off toward low frequency; the -3 dB
_comment.12: point is the soundcard's coupling corner. THIS sets the crossover
_comment.13: biquad f0 in steering_fsp_soundcard.json (match the piplate lowpass
_comment.14: corner to it so the two bands sum ~flat).
_comment.15: * MID-BAND AUTHORITY (K, flat region above the corner): compare to the
_comment.16: piplate x bode at the same amplitude to set aylp_alsa scale so the
_comment.17: AC and DC paths have matched deflection-per-command.
_comment.18: * LINEARITY: the amplitude2 half-amplitude re-sweep flags >10% |H|
_comment.19: change = the card is clipping (line-out overdriven) -> lower
_comment.21: * HF ceiling: where |H| falls off at the top tells the usable AC band.
_comment.22: PREREQ: build/aylp_alsa.so must be built (needs libasound2-dev), and the
_comment.23: sound card line-out must be physically wired into the FSM drive.
_comment.24: SAFETY: bring amplitude/analog-gain up from small; watch the beam on the
_comment.25: 64730 viewer. For the y/tip axis: set bode_plot element 0 and aylp_alsa
_comment.26: scale [1,0] (y sign is +, piplate ch0 +2), output_file ...soundcard_y.

params._roi_note: ROI centred on beam at coarse bias 2/2, matches conf_bode_x.json. Re-derive via contrib/probe_frame.json only on an alignment change.

pipeline._comment: frame -> UDP viewer so you can watch the beam during the sweep

params._note: tracked 20x20 window, threshold 22 -- identical to conf_bode_x.json so this sweep measures the same sensor the loop sees.

pipeline._comment: Replace the x/tilt element (1) with the swept sine; read CoM x as response

params._freq_range: start LOW (2 Hz) to capture the AC-coupling high-pass corner, which the piplate (DC-coupled) sweep never showed. End 300 Hz for the usable AC band and phase slope.
params._tuning: characterization pass: fewer cycles/points than the loop-tuning bode (this is about the SHAPE + corner, not a precision K). Raise n_cycles/n_freqs later if a clean K is wanted.
params._amplitude2: half-amplitude re-sweep: if |H| moves >10% between the two, the line-out is clipping or the CoM left its linear region.
params._k_max_freq: K fit window: keep it ABOVE the expected AC corner so the flat-plateau authority is measured, not the low-freq rolloff. 100 Hz stays below the x ~130 Hz resonance.
params.config: SOUNDCARD x/tilt path: aylp_alsa (hw:0,0, S16_LE, 48 kHz) -> analog sum -> FSM, coarse bias 2.0/2.0, 32x32 exp250 gain100 bw100. Piplate parked (scale 0) holding DC bias only.
params._start_delay: hold injection at 0 (soundcard silent, piplate holding 2 V bias) for 30 s so the coarse channels settle before the sweep.

pipeline._note: SOUNDCARD under test = the AC actuator. scale [0,-1]: drive ONLY x (element 1), muted y; sign mirrors the piplate ch1 (-2). channels 2 = [y,x]. Small buffer/period for low latency; watch the log for underruns.

Piplate PARKED: scale [0,0,0,0] drives nothing; it stays only to hold the DC bias offset (2 V on the fine channels) so the FSM sits in its linear range while the AC-coupled soundcard sweeps. Same port/mapping as conf_bode_x.json.
